In addition, six out of nine files modified here does not have the executable permission set.  For three that does (`numpy/f2py/crackfortran.py`, `numpy/f2py/f2py2e.py`, `numpy/f2py/rules.py`) the permission is removed in this PR.

Here are reasons why the shebang in these files is wrong:
```
$ MKLROOT=/tmp numpy/distutils/system_info.py > /dev/null
/data/builds/numpy/numpy/distutils/system_info.py:192: DeprecationWarning:

  `numpy.distutils` is deprecated since NumPy 1.23.0, as a result
  of the deprecation of `distutils` itself. It will be removed for
  Python >= 3.12. For older Python versions it will remain present.
  It is recommended to use `setuptools < 60.0` for those Python versions.
  For more details, see:
    https://numpy.org/devdocs/reference/distutils_status_migration.html


  from numpy.distutils import log
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/distutils/system_info.py", line 3268, in <module>
    show_all()
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/distutils/system_info.py", line 3259, in show_all
    conf = c()
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/distutils/system_info.py", line 1297, in __init__
    from .cpuinfo import cpu
ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package
$ numpy/f2py/__init__.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/f2py/__init__.py", line 19, in <module>
    from . import f2py2e
ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package
$ numpy/f2py/cfuncs.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/f2py/cfuncs.py", line 16, in <module>
    from . import __version__
ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package
$ numpy/f2py/crackfortran.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/f2py/crackfortran.py", line 154, in <module>
    from . import __version__
ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package
$ numpy/f2py/f2py2e.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/f2py/f2py2e.py", line 20, in <module>
    from . import crackfortran
ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package
$ numpy/f2py/rules.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/builds/numpy/numpy/f2py/rules.py", line 57, in <module>
    from . import __version__
ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package

            ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax

bash: numpy/random/c_distributions.pxd: cannot execute: required file not found
$

If there is a warning or error issued with `-std=c90` then either newer C standard is required to compile or there is a bug in a header.  Either in system one, or in numpy.

Should I update this PR to add the `defined()` guard to the `npy_atomic.h` file?  Or is somewhere specified that C99 (or newer) is needed?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2440004846/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

FAILED numpy/lib/tests/test_function_base.py::TestPercentile::test_linear_interpolation[averaged_inverted_cdf-False-27.5-longdouble-longdouble-quantile-0.4]
```

### Reproduce the code example:

Run NumPy tests on OpenIndiana.

Please note that `/usr/share/jupyter` and `/usr/local/share/jupyter` does not point to the same target so pure `realpath()` cannot solve the issue.  On default OpenIndiana install the `/usr/local` does not exist.  On my test machine it is a symlink to `/opt/local` as documented [here](https://illumos.org/man/7/filesystem) so with [5f24503](https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter_core/pull/375/commits/5f24503c88815e9ea1d9f6c710b83b30ed0aee7c) the failure of `test_jupyter_path_user_site` is this now:

I'm packaging `ipython` for OpenIndiana and I just tried to run tests for the newly packaged (updated) ipython 8.13.1.  Previously with earlier versions of `ipython` packaged for OpenIndiana nobody bothered to run tests and/or to analyze/report failures.

It is possible that this is not a problem with ipython itself but instead a bug in our platform.  The only problem is that I do not know how to prove either.

Please note that I package other Python projects for OpenIndiana too ([hundreds](https://github.com/OpenIndiana/oi-userland/tree/oi/hipster/components/python)) and I never saw similar failure before, IIRC.  This proves nothing, of course,  just FYI.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipython/issues/comments/1534361928/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
